channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: U.S., Iranian Forces Target Civilian Infrastructure
item.description: Attacks on bridges, power plants, and desalination facilities could be considered war crimes.

item.title: Spain and Argentina Share a Common Language and Love for Soccer—but Not Much Else
item.description: Their teams will face off in the upcoming FIFA Men’s World Cup final.

item.title: The Climate Issue newsletter: The dangers of global dimming
item.description: Oliver Morton, our planetary affairs editor, reflects on a warming Earth

item.title: Beijing Is Trying to Turn Taiwan’s Sea Goddess Into a Chinese Agent
item.description: China wants to use the ancient deity Mazu to push unification narratives.

item.title: The Battle for Borsch
item.description: Ukraine is using the beet-based soup to fortify national identity in the face of Russian aggression.

item.title: The Soccer Surveillance State
item.description: How the United States used the World Cup as a testing ground for policing technologies.

item.title: Inside the Race to Protect Ukrainian
item.description: Kyiv’s language revitalization program seeks to save Ukrainians’ mother tongue through complex state law.

item.title: What in the World?
item.description: Test yourself on the week of July 11: China introduces AI regulations, Trump briefly imposes a toll on Hormuz, and Congo’s Ebola outbreak spreads.

item.title: Syria’s Weak Parliament Still Matters
item.description: The country’s newly opened assembly is by no means democratic, but it can still be more than a rubber stamp.

item.title: Cover Story newsletter: It’s too darn hot
item.description: An exclusive look at how we designed our cover

item.title: The U.S. and Iran Signed a Memorandum of Misunderstanding
item.description: Detail and care matter in peacemaking, but not to this White House.

item.title: Does Cuba Really Have Iranian Drones?
item.description: Experts say Trump is laying the pretext for U.S. military action against the island.

item.title: An Alternative History for America at 250
item.description: Commemorating the 250th anniversary of the United States requires an honest appraisal of not only its founding principles but also its racist and imperialist legacy. A clear-eyed assessment shows why many people in the Global South remain cynical about the country’s ability to live up to its ideals.

item.title: The EU’s Contradictory Narratives on China
item.description: Both sides are trying to sustain trade talks and practical cooperation, but the EU’s new security definition of China may complicate this progress.

item.title: Europe Still Has Leverage Over China
item.description: Brussels should use the leverage before it slips away.

item.title: Should you microdose GLP-1 drugs for weight loss?
item.description: Possibly. But only under medical supervision

item.title: Is Taiwan Pinning Its Strategy on an Outdated Vision of America?
item.description: Amid generational shifts and Trump-era transactionalism in the U.S., Taiwan should take a page from Singapore’s book.

item.title: Lebanon’s Peacekeepers Are Heading for the Exits
item.description: As UNIFIL leave Lebanon, it’s not clear who—if anyone—will replace it.

item.title: Pakistan Adopts Wait-and-Watch Approach as US and Iran Return to Hostilities
item.description: Pakistan continues to view the Islamabad MoU that it facilitated as the most viable framework for de-escalation.

item.title: U.S.-China Relations Are More Volatile Than Ever
item.description: Trump and Xi have brutally undermined the institutions and personnel responsible for safeguarding diplomacy.

item.title: Papua New Guinea Just Closed Taiwan’s Economic Office
item.description: Taiwan’s diplomatic space is shrinking along a vital Pacific energy route.

item.title: Boomers have the good life, but it could be longer
item.description: A single chart shows why they have missed out on extra years

item.title: A Turning Point for African Industrialization
item.description: Building on the success of his mega-refinery in Lagos, Nigerian industrialist Aliko Dangote is in talks to construct a second one in collaboration with several East African countries. African leaders should learn from Dangote’s strategy of adding value to the continent’s natural resources and fostering cross-border cooperation.

item.title: What India’s Operationalization of Integrated Battle Groups Means for Pakistan
item.description: Since the inception of the IBGs, they have been framed as formations to launch offensive operations against Pakistan under the nuclear overhang. Now they are finally a reality.

item.title: Pete Hegseth’s testosterone-screening plan is unusual, not crazy
item.description: Soldiers seem to be at particular risk of hypogonadism

item.title: Subsidies Do Not Explain China’s Competitiveness
item.description: Like every major economy, China uses industrial policy, and its subsidies have mattered. But subsidies are no longer the most convincing explanation for Chinese firms’ emergence as global leaders in industries that used to be the exclusive domain of advanced economies.

item.title: How to Isolate Tehran
item.description: Opening Hormuz requires marrying force with effective diplomacy.

item.title: Progress on the China-Kyrgyzstan-Uzbekistan Railway, Some Challenges Remain
item.description: Under discussion for 20 years, the CKU Railway is finally under construction with some hurdles surpassed and other challenges ahead.

item.title: China, North Korea Exchange High-Level Visits
item.description: The visits extend a string of high-level exchanges as Beijing works to keep pace with Pyongyang’s deepening ties with Moscow.

item.title: EU-Japan-South Korea Cooperation Is Indispensable Now
item.description: The trilateral is more feasible – and more important – than ever before.

item.title: Latin America’s Oil Boom
item.description: Some exporters are reaping financial benefits amid the Iran war.

item.title: Putin Likes His Worst Option in Ukraine
item.description: Faced with a quagmire in eastern Ukraine and escalating Ukrainian attacks within Russia, Vladimir Putin must either seek a ceasefire or pursue some form of escalation. With all signs pointing to him embracing the latter alternative, the situation could worsen dramatically for everyone, but especially for him.

item.title: Russia’s Arms Buyers Rejoice Over Trump’s Deal With Turkey
item.description: India and other Kremlin customers will benefit from an easing of restrictions.

item.title: The Subversion of the International Criminal Court
item.description: Already under US sanctions for his role in securing an arrest warrant for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, the chief prosecutor of the International Criminal Court is now at risk of being removed altogether. If member states do not stand up for judicial independence now, they may never get another chance.

item.title: What Inequality Does to Electoral Democracy
item.description: Like many advanced democracies, France has long cycled between periods in which its politics were divided along traditional left-right redistributionist lines, and those in which culture war issues took center stage. If there is any lesson to be drawn from this history, it is that the cycle may be about to repeat.

item.title: Is India Planning an Exchange of Territory With Myanmar?
item.description: The territory that could be considered for exchange lies in Manipur’s Chandel district in India, contiguous to Myanmar’s Kabaw Valley in Sagaing Region.

item.title: The Intensifying Geopolitics of Southeast Asia’s Military Modernization
item.description: At a time of growing regional turbulence, nations across the region are seeking to equip themselves with more and better armaments.

item.title: Philippines Issues Formal Protest to China Over ‘Dehumanizing’ State Media Video
item.description: The Department of Foreign Affairs demanded the "immediate takedown” of the AI-generated video, in which the Philippines was depicted as a monkey.

item.title: America’s Trillion-Dollar Failure
item.description: The Pentagon is broken, but Congress can fix it.

item.title: A Dangerous Game in the Arctic
item.description: Trump’s demands could derail progress on allied defense.

item.title: More Than 500 Feared Dead After Reported Shipwrecks Off Myanmar’s Coast, UN Says
item.description: The reports come on top of the 300 people already reported missing or drowned in the Andaman Sea and Bay of Bengal in 2026.

item.title: New Zealand’s Trade Agreement With India Reflects a Foreign Policy Shift
item.description: The nation is looking to expand and balance its economic and security relationships – and India is at the top of the list.

item.title: The State of Cambodia’s Online Scamming Industry
item.description: A conversation with journalist and advocate Nathan Paul Southern.
